questions.title: Aura visual style
questions.context: Current slowfield aura is a static Graphics circle (Turret.js:232 — fillCircle + strokeCircle in cyan 0x44ddff, 12% fill / 30% stroke). VFX-04 requires a 'visible energy field, not static circle'. This will be the first persistent emitter — Phase 3 only used one-shot bursts.

options.label: Orbiting particles
options.description: Small particles orbit the turret in a circle at the aura radius. Continuous, mesmerizing, clearly shows the field boundary. 8-12 particles per slowfield.

options.label: Radial pulse waves
options.description: Periodic bursts of particles that expand outward from turret center to the edge of the range, like sonar pings. Rhythmic, lower sustained particle count.

options.label: Floating drift field
options.description: Particles spawn randomly within the aura radius and drift slowly inward/outward with gentle turbulence. Creates a foggy, ambient energy feel. Closest to 'flowing energy field'.

questions.title: Aura color
questions.context: Current aura uses 0x44ddff (cyan). Phase 1 established the Void Ethereal palette (deep purples #2d1b4e, #4b2c62, #6a4c93, accent #eef2ff). Should the slowfield aura match the cosmic palette or keep its distinct cyan identity?

options.label: Keep cyan (0x44ddff)
options.description: Maintains the distinct slowfield identity — cyan reads as 'ice/slow' and contrasts well with purple nebula background. Phase 7 will handle full color migration.

options.label: Cosmic purple-blue tints
options.description: Use THEME nebula colors (0x6a4c93, 0x9966ff) to match the cosmic palette immediately. Cohesive but less visually distinct from other effects.

questions.title: Upgraded slowfield aura change
questions.context: Upgraded slowfield already gets increased range (128→160px, Turret.js:265). Should the particle aura also change visually to signal the upgrade?

options.label: Bigger radius only
options.description: Particles simply cover the expanded range. No other visual change — keeps it simple. Upgrade is shown by larger coverage area.

options.label: Bigger radius + denser particles
options.description: More particles (1.5x count) in the expanded radius. Upgraded feels more powerful, slightly higher perf cost.

options.label: Bigger radius + brighter/different tint
options.description: Expanded radius plus a brighter accent color or added glow to visually distinguish upgraded from base. Clear visual feedback.

questions.title: Trail visual approach
questions.context: Current drawLightningChain() at Turret.js:181 draws a simple 2px purple line (0xaa44ff) from turret tip through each chained target, destroyed after 200ms. VFX-06 says 'glow trail particles along its path'. The trail needs to feel like residual energy left behind.

options.label: Particle trail along path
options.description: Spawn 3-5 particles at intervals along each segment of the lightning chain. Particles fade out over ~300ms after the line disappears. Adds lingering sparkle to the chain path.

options.label: Wider glowing line with fade
options.description: Replace the 2px line with a thicker (4-6px) semi-transparent line that fades over 400ms instead of abruptly disappearing at 200ms. Simpler, lower particle cost, still reads as 'glow trail'.

options.label: Both: thick line + particle trail
options.description: Keep the line but make it wider/brighter, AND spawn particles along the path that linger after the line fades. Maximum visual impact, higher particle budget.

questions.title: Trail color treatment
questions.context: Current lightning is solid purple 0xaa44ff. The trail effect could match or contrast to distinguish the 'residual energy' from the initial strike.

options.label: Same purple, lower alpha
options.description: Trail uses 0xaa44ff at reduced opacity (0.4-0.6), fading to 0. Cohesive — the trail is simply the afterimage of the strike.

options.label: Lighter/whiter glow
options.description: Trail particles use accent white (0xeef2ff) or light purple. Creates a 'hot center fading to cool edges' effect — the trail looks like residual heat.

questions.title: Core damage shake intensity
questions.context: SHAKE-01: camera shake on core damage, intensity proportional to damage. Core takes 5 damage (swarmer), 20 (brute/spitter bullet), or 40 (boss). Phaser's camera.shake(duration, intensity) takes a 0-1 float for intensity. SHAKE-04 is handled automatically — UIScene has its own camera.

options.label: Linear scaling with cap
options.description: Map damage linearly to intensity (e.g., 5dmg→0.005, 20dmg→0.02, 40dmg→0.04) with a hard cap at 0.05 to prevent disorientation. Simple and predictable.

options.label: Tiered levels (light/medium/heavy)
options.description: Three fixed shake levels: light (swarmer, 0.005), medium (brute/spitter, 0.015), heavy (boss, 0.04). More controlled — designers can tune each tier independently.

questions.title: Shake duration
questions.context: Shake duration affects game feel significantly. Too short feels like a glitch, too long disrupts aiming. Duration applies to all shake types (core, turret destruction, boss hit).

options.label: Fixed short duration (100-150ms)
options.description: All shakes are brief punchy jolts. Consistent feel, never disrupts gameplay. May feel too subtle for heavy hits.

options.label: Duration scales with intensity
options.description: Light shakes: 80ms, medium: 150ms, heavy: 250ms. Heavier hits feel more impactful but could slightly disrupt turret placement during build phase.

questions.title: Shake stacking behavior
questions.context: Multiple bugs can hit the core in rapid succession (especially swarmers). If 5 swarmers hit in 500ms, should shakes accumulate or does each new shake replace the previous?

options.label: Replace (latest wins)
options.description: Each new shake interrupts the current one. Prevents disorienting sustained vibration during swarmer rushes. Simpler — just call camera.shake() each time.

options.label: Additive with cap
options.description: Shakes accumulate intensity but cap at a maximum (e.g., 0.05). Multiple rapid hits feel increasingly impactful, but can't exceed a comfort threshold.

options.label: Cooldown-based
options.description: Skip shake if one is already playing (cooldown period = shake duration). Prevents overlap entirely. May miss some impacts during intense combat.

questions.title: Boss micro-shake trigger
questions.context: SHAKE-03: boss hit impacts cause micro-shake. Bug.takeDamage() at Bug.js:150 doesn't currently check bug type. The boss has 1500 HP and takes many hits before dying. A micro-shake on every single bullet hit could be very frequent. Where should this trigger?

options.label: Every hit, very subtle (0.002, 50ms)
options.description: Trigger micro-shake on every boss takeDamage(). Extremely subtle — barely perceptible vibration that creates a subliminal 'hitting something big' feel. High frequency, minimal intensity.

options.label: HP threshold milestones
options.description: Shake only when boss crosses 75%/50%/25% HP thresholds. Fewer shakes, each more noticeable. Creates dramatic 'the boss is weakening' moments.

options.label: Cooldown-throttled (every 500ms max)
options.description: Micro-shake on hits but no more than once every 500ms. Balances 'felt impact' with not being annoying. Works well with rapid-fire blasters.
